sections.paragraph: γόης, ητος, ὁ [cp. γόος ‘lamentation’ and γοάω ‘display signs of grief, wail’; since formal lamentation included repetition of wail words, it was a short step from the role of lamenter to that of a ‘sorcerer’, whose specialty was repetitious spells, in short a ‘charlatan’] swindler, imposter 2 Ti 3:13.
